source: National: rice 150.18 MT milled 2024-25 (Agri Ministry) / 0.67 outturn = ~2240 LMT paddy; FCI+state CMR procurement KMS 2024-25 = 832 LMT (DFPD, per omss-processing-returns.md)
note: Base for mass balance. FCI procures PADDY (not rice) and gives it to CMR millers.

source: FCI CMR outturn ratio 67% (CAG/FCI norm); 832 x 0.67 = 558 LMT rice returned to FCI
note: The ONLY stream FCI holds. This is the food grain. Rice-for-ethanol burns THIS (food-vs-fuel).

downstream_use: boiler fuel, power co-gen, silica (RHA 85-97% SiO2), biochar
source: 20-22% of paddy (IRRI Rice Knowledge Bank; DCMSME); calorific 14-16 MJ/kg; RHA silica 85-97% (IOPscience)
note: NON-FOOD energy/silica stream. Retained by CMR miller, never held by FCI. ~183 LMT from procured paddy alone.

downstream_use: rice bran OIL (edible) -> DORB feed -> acid oil/biodiesel
source: 8% of paddy (IRRI 8-12%; FCI/repo 8%); bran value Rs 19/kg (omss model)
note: NON-FOOD oil+feed stream. Part of the Rs 7,650 cr/yr byproduct giveaway. ~67 LMT from procured paddy.

downstream_use: edible RBO (import substitution) then residual biodiesel
source: Bran ~179 LMT x ~16-18% oil = ~29 LMT theoretical; SEA processable bran ~5 MMT/50 LMT -> ~8-9 LMT; potential 1.62 MMT (PIB NMEO); actual RBO ~10-11 LMT (India #1, 36.7% global share 2024)
note: THE GAP: India captures only ~1/3 of national bran-oil potential because ~70% of bran goes to huller mills/feed/degrades (lipase FFA >5-10% within hours) unstabilized. Stabilization+modern extraction is the opportunity.

source: India world's #1 RBO producer, ~10-11 LMT, 36.7% global market share 2024 (NITI Aug 2024; market reports)
note: Actual current output vs ~29 LMT theoretical / ~16 LMT if all processable bran used = large unextracted gap.
